domain: ethology / chemical communication (ant pheromone trail evaporation)
specific_mechanism: Ant species deposit volatile alkanes/alcohols as pheromone trails that linearly decay by evaporation; reinforcement requires repeated trail-laying; if foragers stop reinforcing, the trail vanishes within minutes-to-hours depending on chemical volatility. The system has automatic obsolescence: stale information disappears without explicit deletion
llm_application: An agent-cached recommendation store with built-in volatility: each cached suggestion (heuristic, retrieval shortcut, learned habit) decays linearly with time unless reinforced by repeated use; obsolete cache entries vanish automatically. The chemistry analog is volatility = forgetting rate, reinforcement = use, replenishment = re-laying.
novelty_claim: Existing cache eviction is LRU or fixed-TTL based on access timestamps. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a per-entry chemical-volatility-style continuous decay where the decay rate is itself a parameter analogous to pheromone molecule volatility, with reinforcement (use) replenishing rather than resetting.
